race.name: Colnago GF Mont Ventoux
race.tagline: Conquer the Giant of Provence in a legendary summit finish

climate.description: Early June in Provence features mild Mediterranean conditions with low precipitation risk. Mont Ventoux summit can experience significant wind and cooler temperatures despite warm valley conditions.

terrain.surface: Provence cyclosportive through lavender fields, vineyards, Gorges de la Nesque, and Provençal villages, culminating in a summit finish at Mont Ventoux (1,912 m). Multiple significant climbs including Col de l'Homme Mort and Sault ascent.
features: Provence cyclosportive through lavender fields, vineyards, Gorges de la Nesque, and Provençal villages, culminating in a summit finish at Mont Ventoux (1,912 m)
Multiple significant climbs including Col de l'Homme Mort and Sault ascent

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 76. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 71.

course_description.character: A legendary Provençal gran fondo centered on Mont Ventoux, Europe's most iconic climb. The route traverses stunning landscapes—lavender fields, vineyards, Gorges de la Nesque, and quaint villages—before culminating in a dramatic summit finish at 1,912 m. The event emphasizes both sporting challenge and cultural immersion in Provence.
course_description.signature_challenge: Mont Ventoux summit finish via Bédoin (Granfondo) or Sault (Mediofondo/Piccolofondo). The final ascent is renowned as one of France's most demanding climbs, combining sustained gradient, wind exposure, and psychological challenge of the barren, rocky summit.
suffering_zones: Col de l'Homme Mort: sustained climb draining energy reserves before Mont Ventoux
Sault ascent: significant elevation gain on mediofondo/piccolofondo routes
Mont Ventoux final ascent: the defining challenge with grades up to 17.2%, wind exposure, and altitude effects from Chalet Reynard (1,632 m) to summit (1,912 m)

logistics.airport: MRS (Marseille Provence Airport), 120km, 2hr drive; AVN (Avignon-Provence Airport), 65km, 1hr drive
logistics.lodging_strategy: Vaison-la-Romaine offers hotels and lodging. Sportive Breaks packages include 3 nights on half-board basis with breakfasts and evening meals.
logistics.transport: Event based in Vaison-la-Romaine, a charming Provençal town. Sportive Breaks offers 3-night packages including accommodation, meals, guided rides, and mechanical support. Closed roads on Mont Ventoux from 8:30 AM to 3 PM. Race start 7:30 AM.

history.origin_story: The Colnago GF Mont Ventoux is described as a legendary cyclosportive now in its sixth year (as of 2026), celebrated for world-class organization rivaling top events like Etape du Tour. The event is branded by Colnago and organized in partnership with Ventoux Provence.
notable_moments: Established reputation rivaling Etape du Tour for organization quality
Saturday gravel route additions introduced in recent years
Route alternation system implemented from 2025 (northern/southern bypass variants)
Consistent summit finish at Mont Ventoux (1,912 m) with closed roads and professional support

biased_opinion.summary: The Colnago GF Mont Ventoux is a bucket-list event that delivers on every front: iconic climb, stunning Provençal setting, world-class organization, and genuine suffering. The summit finish at 1,912 m with closed roads and professional support creates a truly memorable experience. Multiple distance options make it accessible to varied abilities. Primary concern is unknown registration costs and limited value transparency, though the included package (accommodation, guides, support) suggests premium positioning.

final_verdict.one_liner: A legendary Mont Ventoux summit finish in Provence with world-class organization and unforgettable scenery—a true bucket-list gran fondo.
final_verdict.should_you_race: The Colnago GF Mont Ventoux ranks among Europe's premier gran fondos. The event combines cycling's most iconic climb with stunning Provençal landscapes, professional-level logistics, and festive community atmosphere. The 135 km Granfondo with 3,144 m elevation delivers genuine challenge, while shorter options and Saturday gravel routes provide accessibility. Closed roads on Mont Ventoux, feed stations, mechanical support, and a summit finish at 1,912 m create a professional-grade experience. The event's reputation rivals Etape du Tour, and the inclusion of accommodation and guided rides in packages adds value for destination travelers. Primary limitations are undisclosed registration costs and limited field-size data. For cyclists seeking a legendary European gran fondo combining sporting challenge with cultural immersion, this event is essential.

videos.title: La Colnago GF Mont Ventoux 2025, Course sur l'un des Hors Catégories les plus dur de France ! Cyclos
videos.description:
FR - 🏁 Course GF Mont-Ventoux 2025 à Vaison la Romaine, 55ème ascensions du Mont-Ventoux.
Départ dans le SAS 2, bien remonté les groupes et les coureurs les trois première heures. Plaisir dans les descentes de Sault et les Gorges de la Nesque.
Arrivé au virage de Saint Estéve en 4h00 et 30 km/h de moyenne, bon travail.
Très chaud dans la partie bois du pied du Ventoux jusque un peu après le chalet Reynard. Une pause obligatoire de 10 minutes au km 124, coup de chaud et cardio anormalement élevé (10 à 15 bpm de trop). C'étais beaucoup mieux après et jusqu'à l'arrivée.
3 arrêts ravito pour remplir les bidons, beaucoup de temps de perdu.

Bon entrainement pour la suite et beaucoup de plaisir et d'amusement sur le vélo 😉 J'avais encore pas super bien récupérer du double Ventoux de mardi mais sa fait partie du job 😅

Pause course jusqu'au 20 juin 🎉, place à l'entrainement et surtout à l'affutage.

Classement :
Scratch : 936 ème sur 2234
Par catégorie M2 (35-44ans) : 229 ème sur 520

Temps : 6h14:31 pour 135,00 km et 3200 m d+
Equipe Nîmes Cyclisme 🐊🔴⚫
